name: Ian Goodfellow
key_contributions: Co-authored the influential "Deep Learning" textbook (2016) with Bengio and Courville
Pioneered adversarial training as a paradigm for generative models
summary: Ian Goodfellow invented Generative Adversarial Networks, one of the most creative ideas in modern machine learning. The legend goes that the idea came to him in a bar in Montreal in 2014. GANs introduced the concept of two neural networks — a generator and a discriminator — training against each other, producing increasingly realistic outputs. This framework enabled the generative AI revolution in images and influenced the development of diffusion models that followed.
